sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins and suffering 2 losses, with a tendency to concede first in most games. Everton, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 3 matches, drawing 4 of their last 5 games, often with low-scoring outcomes. This suggests Fulham may have attacking momentum at home, while Everton’s resilience could make them tough to break down.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Everton have dominated, winning both matches (2-0 at home and 3-1 away). Fulham have struggled to keep clean sheets against Everton, conceding in their last 3 head-to-head games, which indicates Everton might have a psychological edge coming into this match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham, sitting 9th with 34 points, have scored 34 goals but underperformed their expected goals by over 6, showing inefficiency in attack (ranked 19th in xG over/underperformance). Everton, 10th with the same points tally, have a more balanced but less potent attack with 26 goals scored, often playing low-scoring games (5/5 under 2.5 goals recently). Fulham’s home advantage and higher possession stats (51.5%, ranked 9th) could be key against Everton’s defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Fulham, Harry Wilson (7.82 avg rating, 3 goals in last 5) and Samuel Chukwueze (7.3 avg rating, 3 goals) are in fine form and could exploit Everton’s defense. Everton’s James Garner (7.76 avg rating in last 5) has been crucial in midfield, while Jordan Pickford’s consistency in goal (3.4 saves/game in last 5 head-to-heads) could frustrate Fulham’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Fulham’s recent trend of high-scoring games (5/6 over 2.5 goals) contrasts with Everton’s low-scoring streak (5/5 under 2.5 goals), suggesting a clash of styles. Fulham’s vulnerability in conceding first (4/5 matches) might be targeted by Everton’s late-game scoring ability (9 goals in 76-90 mins this season). Expect a tight battle with Fulham pushing for an early lead at Craven Cottage.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 44% chance for a home win, coupled with Fulham’s attacking form at home and Everton’s tendency to draw, a narrow victory for Fulham seems plausible. However, Everton’s recent head-to-head dominance and unbeaten run could lead to a closely contested match, potentially ending in a draw if Fulham fail to convert their chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Fulham lead Everton 1-0 at Craven Cottage. The first half saw Fulham dominate possession and create more scoring opportunities, though they have yet to capitalize fully on their chances. Everton, on the other hand, managed to stay in the game despite an own goal from Vitaliy Mykolenko in the 18th minute, which handed Fulham the lead. Both teams have shown moments of promise, but Fulham appear to have the upper hand going into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Fulham have controlled much of the game, holding the majority of possession and generating a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area. Their attacking intent is evident with multiple big chances created, though they’ve struggled with finishing, missing several key opportunities. Everton, while less dominant in possession, have shown resilience defensively and have had their own moments in attack, creating at least one big chance. Their aerial strength has been a positive, winning more duels in the air compared to Fulham, but they’ll need to be more clinical in the second half to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Fulham, players like Raúl Jiménez and Emile Smith Rowe have been central to their attacking efforts, with both getting into promising positions and testing the Everton defense. Samuel Chukwueze and Ryan Sessegnon have also contributed with creative play on the flanks. On the Everton side, Jordan Pickford has been a standout with crucial interventions to keep his team in the game. Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall has shown creativity in midfield with key passes, while James Garner has worked hard to disrupt Fulham’s rhythm. However, Everton’s forward line, including Thierno Barry, needs to step up after missing a significant chance in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Fulham asserting early pressure, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes. Everton responded with a strong spell of attacking momentum shortly after, maintaining pressure for a sustained period. However, Fulham regained control around the midway point of the half, enjoying a prolonged period of dominance with consistent attacking waves, particularly around the time of the goal. Towards the end of the half, Everton fought back with another spell of pressure, but Fulham finished strongly, showing intent to extend their lead. These shifts suggest both teams have the capacity to create danger, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Fulham’s approach has been to dominate possession and exploit the flanks, using width to stretch Everton’s defense. Their high number of penalty area entries indicates a focus on getting into dangerous positions, though their finishing needs improvement. Everton have relied on a more compact structure, looking to counter when opportunities arise. Their success in aerial duels suggests a potential reliance on set pieces or long balls to create chances. Discipline could be a concern for Everton with an early yellow card, and they’ll need to avoid further bookings to maintain their shape in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Fulham’s first-half dominance and higher expected goals, they are likely to push for a second goal to secure the game. Players like Jiménez and Smith Rowe will be key to converting their chances. Everton, trailing by a single goal, will need to take more risks, potentially opening up the game as they search for an equalizer. Their ability to win possession in the final third could be crucial if they are to turn the game around. Both teams have hit the woodwork, indicating that fine margins could decide the outcome in the second half.
